instructions: 1. Create `src/jitsu/server/handlers.py`. 2. Import necessary dependencies (types, Path, subprocess, re, etc.) that the handlers require. 3. Define a `ToolHandlers` class. 4. In `__init__`, accept `state_manager: JitsuStateManager` and `context_compiler: ContextCompiler`, saving them as instance variables. 5. Move all 8 `_handle_*` functions from `mcp_server.py` into this class as methods (e.g., `async def handle_get_next_phase(self) -> ...`). Update any references to `state_manager` and `context_compiler` to use `self.`. 6. Add a method `def register_all(self, registry: ToolRegistry) -> None:` that calls `registry.register()` for all 8 tools, mapping the exact `types.Tool` schemas from the old implementation to their new `self.handle_*` methods.
anti_patterns: Do NOT modify mcp_server.py in this phase. Only copy the logic.
Do NOT forget to migrate the imports needed by the handlers (like JitsuStateManager, CommandRunner, ValidationError, GitProvider, etc.) into handlers.py.
Do NOT change the tool schemas or behavior; this is a pure refactor.
completion_criteria: `src/jitsu/server/handlers.py` is created with a fully functional `ToolHandlers` class.
All 8 tools are correctly mapped in the `register_all` method.

instructions: 1. Import `ToolHandlers` from `jitsu.server.handlers`. 2. In `mcp_server.py`, immediately below where `tool_registry` is instantiated, instantiate `handlers = ToolHandlers(state_manager, context_compiler)`. 3. Call `handlers.register_all(tool_registry)`. 4. Delete the 8 manual `tool_registry.register(...)` calls you added in the previous epic. 5. Delete all of the old `_handle_*` functions from `mcp_server.py`. 6. Clean up all orphaned imports at the top of `mcp_server.py` (e.g., `subprocess`, `re`, `CommandRunner`, `PhaseReport`, etc., which are no longer used here).
anti_patterns: Do NOT leave any dead code or unused imports in mcp_server.py. Pyright strict mode will fail if you do.
Do NOT delete the `run_server`, `handle_list_tools`, or `handle_call_tool` functions.
completion_criteria: `mcp_server.py` is stripped of all domain execution logic.
`mcp_server.py` cleanly delegates handler registration to the `ToolHandlers` class.
